hawkish_phrases: further gradual increases in the target range for the federal funds rate will be consistent with sustained expansion
another increase in the target range for the federal funds rate was likely to be warranted fairly soon
tightening resource utilization...could generate undesirable upward pressure on inflation
measures of nominal wage growth appeared to be picking up
dovish_phrases: growth of business fixed investment had moderated from its rapid pace earlier in the year
downside risks for domestic economic growth and inflation
a couple of participants noted that the federal funds rate might currently be near its neutral level
conditions remain depressed...due to the effects of trade policy actions
summary: The FOMC unanimously held rates at 2.00-2.25% in November 2018 with explicit guidance that another increase was likely warranted fairly soon, maintaining a clear tightening bias despite some moderation in business investment and emerging downside risks.
